county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Upton County formed 1887 from Tom Green. County seat is Rankin. West Texas / Permian Basin region with ranching, oil, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Rankin. West Texas county organized 1910. Upton County Historical Commission. Oil and ranching. Check Tom Green for pre-organization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Upton County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1887; County Clerk in Rankin holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Upton County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1887 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Upton County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1887. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Upton County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1887. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Upton County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1887 to 1950 for Upton County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Upton County Oil and Gas Lease Records -- GLO / County Clerk
source_profiles.repository: Texas General Land Office, Austin / County Clerk, Rankin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease records, mineral rights transfers, and related land documents from 1920. Texas GLO maintains state mineral records. County Clerk has private mineral lease records.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Upton County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Upton County from 1887. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Uvalde County formed 1850 from Bexar. County seat is Uvalde. Texas Hill Country region with German, Czech, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Uvalde. Southwest Texas county. John Nance Garner Memorial Museum (VP under FDR). Fort Inge (frontier military post). Uvalde County Historical Commission. Check Bexar for pre-1850 records. Ranching heritage. Significant Mexican-American community.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Uvalde County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1850; County Clerk in Uvalde holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1850. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Uvalde County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Uvalde County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Uvalde County Area (Apache and Comanche)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for Apache and Comanche in the Uvalde County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Uvalde County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Uvalde County from 1850. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Val Verde County formed 1885 from Crockett, Kinney, Pecos. County seat is Del Rio. West Texas / Permian Basin region with ranching, oil, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Del Rio. Southwest Texas border county. Whitehead Memorial Museum. Val Verde Winery (oldest winery in Texas). Seminole Canyon State Park (prehistoric pictographs). Laughlin Air Force Base records. Check Crockett, Kinney, Pecos for pre-1885 records. Border crossing records at NARA.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Val Verde County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1885; County Clerk in Del Rio holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Van Zandt County formed 1848 from Henderson. County seat is Canton. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Canton. Northeast Texas county. Van Zandt County Historical Commission. Canton First Monday Trade Days heritage. Check Henderson for pre-1848 records. Agricultural community.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Victoria County formed 1836 from Original Texas county (Municipality of Victoria). County seat is Victoria. Gulf Coast region with port, plantation, and petrochemical heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Victoria. Original Texas county — one of the oldest continuously settled areas (Spanish colonial heritage, De Leon's colony). Victoria County Historical Commission. Museum of the Coastal Bend. De Leon Colony records at GLO and TSLAC. Catholic sacramental records from Spanish era. Significant German, Czech, and Mexican-American heritage. McNamara House Museum.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Victoria County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1836; County Clerk in Victoria holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Victoria County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1836 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Victoria County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1836. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Victoria County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1836. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Victoria County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Victoria County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Spanish Colonial / Mission Records -- Victoria County Area
source_profiles.repository: Catholic Archives of San Antonio / TSLAC / Bexar Archives at UT-Austin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Spanish colonial records including mission registers (baptisms, marriages, burials), presidio records, and land grants. Victoria County area was part of the Spanish colonial system. Records at Catholic Archives of San Antonio, TSLAC, and Bexar Archives at UT-Austin.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Colonial-era records vary in survival; many originals at Catholic Archives or in Mexican archives

source_profiles.source_name: Victoria County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Victoria County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Victoria County Land Grants
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Victoria County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Walker County formed 1846 from Montgomery. County seat is Huntsville. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Huntsville. East Texas county. Sam Houston Memorial Museum in Huntsville. Texas prison system (Huntsville Unit) records — TDCJ archives. Sam Houston State University archives. Check Montgomery for pre-1846 records. Significant African American community.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Walker County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Huntsville holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Waller County formed 1873 from Austin, Grimes. County seat is Hempstead. Gulf Coast region with port, plantation, and petrochemical heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Hempstead. Southeast Texas county. Waller County Historical Museum. Prairie View A&M University (historically Black university) — university archives. Liendo Plantation heritage. Check Austin and Grimes for pre-1873 records. Freedmen's communities.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Waller County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1873; County Clerk in Hempstead holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Washington County formed 1836 from Original Texas county (Municipality of Washington). County seat is Brenham. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Brenham. Original Texas county — Washington-on-the-Brazos (where Texas Declaration of Independence was signed, 1836). Star of the Republic Museum. Washington County Historical Society. Some of the most important early Texas records. Major plantation county. Significant African American heritage. Bluebell Creameries heritage. Check for Republic-era records at TSLAC.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Washington County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1836; County Clerk in Brenham holds deed books

source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / TSLAC / Texas Lutheran University archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German immigrant church records including baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Washington County has significant German settlement heritage. German-language records may require translation. Check local Lutheran, Catholic, and Methodist congregations.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Webb County formed 1848 from Bexar, Nueces. County seat is Laredo. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Laredo. South Texas border county. Webb County Heritage Foundation. Republic of the Rio Grande Museum. Laredo was under seven flags. Significant Mexican-American heritage. Catholic sacramental records from Spanish era. Border crossing records at NARA. Check Bexar and Nueces for pre-1848 records. Texas A&M International University archives.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Webb County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1848; County Clerk in Laredo holds deed books
